text: The surgeon performs a four-centimeter peri-umbilical incision and inserts a wound protector for specimen extraction and anastomosis. A sterile probe cover is placed on the ultrasound probe, and the small bowel is irrigated with sterile saline using a bladder syringe to facilitate acoustic coupling.
The ultrasound imaging reveals thickening of the mucosa and submucosa in the terminal ileum and ileocecal region, with visible skip lesions in the small bowel.

The ultrasound image shows a comparison between normal small bowel at the top and inflamed, thickened small bowel at the bottom. The surgical procedure includes a limited ileocecal resection with a side-to-side ileocolic anastomosis and three Heineken-Mikulitz strictureplasties.
